_comment: Sanitized starter config. Copy to crabmeat.json and edit. Secrets (gateway.auth.token, admin.token) are injected from .env at load time — leave them out of this file.
_providers_note: Default ships an Anthropic stub — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env. To use OpenAI, swap type to 'openai' and the env-var ref to $SECRET:OPENAI_API_KEY (model e.g. 'gpt-4o-mini'). To use a local Ollama, replace this block with the example in 'providers (local Ollama)' below and set allowLocalProviders=true (it already is).
_providers_local_llamacpp_alternate_note: llama.cpp's bundled server (llama-server) speaks the OpenAI API, so it uses type 'openai' — no dedicated provider needed. Context size is set when the server is launched (-c flag), not per-request, so the Ollama num_ctx pitfall does not apply. Embedding shells (e.g. PolyGlot) launch llama-server themselves and point a provider here.
_tools_note: The agent can only call tools listed here AND in the agent's 'tools' array. This catalog mirrors CrabMeat's built-in tool handlers; trim it if you want a narrower agent.

tools.name: file_read
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file inside the workspace, optionally a line range.

path.description: Path to the file to read, inside the workspace.

offset.description: 1-based line number to start reading from.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return.

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Atomically create or overwrite a text file inside the workspace.

path.description: Path to the file to write, inside the workspace.

overwrite.description: Must be true to replace an existing file.

dry_run.description: Preview the write without touching disk.

tools.name: file_edit
tools.description: Replace an exact string in an existing workspace text file.

path.description: Path to the existing file to edit.

old_string.description: Exact text to find; must be non-empty and present in the file.

new_string.description: Replacement text; must differ from old_string.

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of requiring a single unique match.

tools.name: file_list
tools.description: List directory entries (name, type, size) for a workspace directory.

path.description: Directory path to list, inside the workspace.

tools.name: file_move
tools.description: Move one or more files to a destination inside the workspace.

source.description: Single source file path; use this or 'sources'.

sources.description: Array of source file paths; use this or 'source'.

dry_run.description: Preview the move plan; returns a confirm_token.

confirm_token.description: Token from a prior preview, required to execute bulk moves.

tools.name: file_copy
tools.description: Copy one or more files to a destination inside the workspace.

source.description: Single source file path; use this or 'sources'.

sources.description: Array of source file paths; use this or 'source'.

dry_run.description: Preview the copy plan; returns a confirm_token.

confirm_token.description: Token from a prior preview, required to execute bulk copies.

tools.name: glob_search
tools.description: Find files by glob pattern (*, **, ?, {a,b}) under a workspace directory.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match file paths against.

tools.name: grep_search
tools.description: Search file contents by regular expression under a workspace path.

pattern.description: JavaScript regular expression to match against each line.

glob.description: Glob filter limiting which files are scanned.

files_only.description: Return only matching file paths instead of lines.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Find files by name or content with one plain-text query. No regex or glob syntax — query tokens are matched against file names and content lines, results merged and ranked.

tools.name: launch_app
tools.description: Open an installed desktop application by name. Resolution is deterministic (learned aliases, then registry match with a confidence gate); ambiguous names return candidates instead of launching. Owner-only.

name.description: App name or the user's phrasing for it.

alias.description: Original user phrasing to remember for this app after a successful launch.

dry_run.description: Resolve and report what would launch without launching.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web (Tavily/Brave/DuckDuckGo, auto-selected by configured keys).

days.description: Recency window in days for news searches (1-30).

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a public HTTP/HTTPS URL and return its text content.

maxLength.description: Maximum characters of body text to return.

tools.name: weather
tools.description: Look up a weather report for a location.

tools.name: pdf_extract
tools.description: Extract and normalize text from a .pdf file inside the workspace.

path.description: Path to the .pdf file, inside the workspace.

max_chars.description: Maximum characters of extracted text to return.

tools.name: timer
tools.description: Per-session stopwatch. Start, check, stop, or list named timers.

tools.name: random
tools.description: Generate random values: integer, float, uuid, choice, or dice.

options.description: Items to pick from in choice mode.

count.description: Number of dice to roll in dice mode.

sides.description: Number of sides per die in dice mode.

tools.name: memory_read
tools.description: Read a saved memory note by key, or list all keys when no key is given.

key.description: Memory key to read; omit to list all keys.

tools.name: memory_write
tools.description: Persist a memory note to disk under a key.

key.description: Memory key (letters, numbers, dashes, underscores, dots).

tools.name: todo_write
tools.description: In-session planning scratchpad: list, set, update, or clear the todo list.

todos.description: For 'set': replacement list of todo items.

id.description: For 'update': id of the todo item to modify.

text.description: For 'update': new text for the todo item.

tools.name: ask_user
tools.description: Pause and ask the user a question mid-task, then wait for the reply.

question.description: Question to put to the user.

options.description: Up to 8 option strings for a multiple-choice prompt.

allow_freeform.description: Whether the user may answer with free text.

timeout_ms.description: How long to wait for a reply, in milliseconds.

tools.name: plan_mode
tools.description: Planning checkpoint: enter read-only plan mode, exit with a structured plan, or query status.

plan.description: For 'exit': structured plan with goal, steps, and confidence.

tools.name: shell
tools.description: Run a shell command on the host with an allowlisted environment.

cwd.description: Working directory; must be inside the workspace or an allowed access root.

dry_run.description: Report what would run without executing.

agents.name: CrabMeat Agent
agents.systemPrompt: You are a capable AI agent running through CrabMeat. Use the tools available to you to investigate, plan, and execute tasks. For file work, prefer the dedicated file and search tools over running shell commands — they are faster, cross-platform, and avoid quoting pitfalls. Explore with read-only tools first, and plan before write or exec actions on non-trivial work.

_agents_gui_shell_alternate_note: Minimal tool surface for thin GUI shells driven by small local models (1.7B-4B class). Translating short utterances into one of ~5 tools is a fair fight for those models; the full catalog above is not. Pair with a local provider and keep the catalog this small.

_agents_gui_shell_alternate.name: Desktop Assistant
_agents_gui_shell_alternate.systemPrompt: You are a friendly desktop assistant. Translate what the user asks into one tool call. If a tool result asks you to clarify with the user, relay that question in plain language and wait. Never invent capabilities you don't have.

_fileAccess_note: fileAccessPresets safely grants common user folders under your home directory. fileAccessPaths is for additional explicit absolute roots only, e.g. ["C:/Users/me/Projects", "D:/Media/Inbox"]. Do not use drive roots, home itself, or relative paths; the schema rejects them.
_emailImap_note: Inbound+outbound email connector. Credentials must NOT live here — put them in .crabmeat/local.json (gitignored), which deep-merges over this file at boot. Schema: connectors.emailImap = { user, password (Gmail App Password), from?, allowFromAddresses[] (required), outboundTo? (defaults to allowFromAddresses), imapHost?, imapPort?, smtpHost?, smtpPort?, pollIntervalMs?, mailbox? }. See README 'Email Agent' for the cold-start walk-through.
_refusalFallback_note: Interception layer: when the primary model refuses, CrabMeat silently reroutes to a fallback local provider. Two modes: (1) strict — only reroute requests whose content class is on contentClassAllowlist (nsfw-search, adult-creative, edgy-humor, security-research, substance-info); (2) rerouteUnclassified=true — also reroute when the classifier couldn't identify a class (the 'no babysitter' mode — catches small local models refusing benign queries). Disabled by default.
